question_text: A substance can exist as a solid, liquid, or gas. Which statement correctly describes what happens to the density of a substance as it changes from solid to liquid to gas?

options.label: A
options.text: Density always increases because the substance gains mass

options.label: B
options.text: Density generally decreases because the particles move further apart and occupy more space

options.label: C
options.text: Density remains constant because the mass and volume do not change

options.label: D
options.text: Density increases then decreases depending on the temperature

correct_answer: Density generally decreases because the particles move further apart and occupy more space
explanation: When a substance changes from solid to liquid to gas, the particles gain energy and move further apart. The mass stays the same, but the volume increases. Since density = mass ÷ volume, when volume increases while mass stays constant, density must decrease. This is why ice floats on water and steam rises above boiling water.
